site: AI Centre of Excellence Regulatory List
tagline: Curated regulatory guidances, standards & best practices

pages.title: Aerospace & defense — airworthiness, space & export control
pages.intro: A curated index of authoritative, primary-source regulations, certification specifications, and standards governing aircraft airworthiness, aviation safety, aerospace quality, space systems, and defense trade and export control. Every link points to the issuing body's own canonical page or document.

pages.title: AI governance & responsible AI — regulation, frameworks & standards
pages.intro: A curated index of authoritative, primary-source law, risk frameworks, management standards, and international principles governing the responsible development and use of artificial intelligence. Every link points to the issuing body's own canonical page or document.

entries.notes: The world's first comprehensive AI law; risk-based rules phasing in through 2 Aug 2027
entries.applies_to: providers, deployers, and other operators of AI systems placed on the EU market, with risk-tiered obligations phasing in through 2027

entries.notes: Commission overview of the AI Act's risk tiers, obligations, and timeline
entries.applies_to: organizations seeking an official explanation of the AI Act's risk tiers, obligations, and implementation timeline

entries.notes: Commission body enforcing the AI Act and supervising general-purpose AI models
entries.applies_to: providers of general-purpose AI models subject to EU AI Act enforcement and supervision

entries.document: Executive Order 14179 — Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
entries.notes: Signed 23 Jan 2025; revokes EO 14110 (30 Oct 2023) and resets US federal AI policy
entries.applies_to: US federal agencies and federal AI policy direction

entries.document: OMB M-24-10 — Advancing Governance, Innovation, and Risk Management for Agency Use of AI
entries.notes: Set minimum risk-management practices for federal AI use; superseded by M-25-21
entries.applies_to: US federal agencies' risk-management practices for their own use of AI

entries.document: OMB M-25-21 — Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust
entries.notes: Current federal agency AI governance memorandum; replaces M-24-10
entries.applies_to: US federal agencies' current governance requirements for adopting and using AI

entries.document: Colorado AI Act — SB 24-205, Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence
entries.notes: First comprehensive US state AI law; duties on developers/deployers of high-risk AI; effective 1 Feb 2026
entries.applies_to: developers and deployers of high-risk AI systems doing business in Colorado

entries.document: AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0), NIST AI 100-1
entries.notes: Voluntary framework structured around Govern, Map, Measure, Manage; released 26 Jan 2023
entries.applies_to: organizations voluntarily managing AI risk across the Govern, Map, Measure, Manage functions

entries.document: AI RMF: Generative AI Profile, NIST AI 600-1
entries.notes: Companion profile listing 12 GenAI risks and 200-plus actions; released 26 Jul 2024
entries.applies_to: organizations applying the AI RMF specifically to generative AI risks and mitigations

entries.notes: Hosts the AI RMF Playbook, crosswalks, and trustworthy-AI technical resources
entries.applies_to: organizations seeking implementation resources for the NIST AI RMF and trustworthy-AI practices

entries.notes: Certifiable AI management-system standard (Plan-Do-Check-Act); catalogue page, paid
entries.applies_to: organizations seeking a certifiable management system for responsibly developing or using AI

entries.document: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — Guidance on AI risk management
entries.notes: Guidance (non-certifiable) adapting ISO 31000 risk management to AI; catalogue page, paid
entries.applies_to: organizations adapting ISO 31000 risk management practices to AI systems

entries.notes: Foundational vocabulary underpinning the ISO/IEC AI standards suite; catalogue page, paid
entries.applies_to: organizations needing common vocabulary and concepts across the ISO/IEC AI standards suite

entries.notes: Repeatable process for documenting AI impacts across the lifecycle; catalogue page, paid
entries.applies_to: organizations documenting AI system impacts across the development and deployment lifecycle

entries.document: ISO/IEC 38507:2022 — Governance implications of the use of AI by organizations
entries.notes: Board-level governance guidance for organizational AI use; catalogue page, paid
entries.applies_to: governing bodies and boards overseeing organizational use of AI

entries.notes: First intergovernmental AI standard; adopted 2019, updated May 2024
entries.applies_to: governments and organizations adopting the first intergovernmental AI policy standard

entries.notes: Live repository of national AI policies, the AI incidents monitor, and metrics
entries.applies_to: policymakers and researchers tracking national AI policies, incidents, and metrics

entries.document: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
entries.notes: First global standard on AI ethics; adopted by 193 Member States, Nov 2021
entries.applies_to: UNESCO member states adopting a shared global standard on AI ethics

entries.document: Hiroshima Process International Code of Conduct for Advanced AI Systems
entries.notes: Voluntary 11-point code for organizations developing advanced AI; published 30 Oct 2023
entries.applies_to: organizations developing advanced AI systems, voluntarily adopting an 11-point code of conduct

entries.document: Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law (CETS No. 225)
entries.notes: First legally binding international AI treaty; opened for signature 5 Sep 2024
entries.applies_to: states party to the Council of Europe treaty binding them to AI human-rights and rule-of-law safeguards

entries.document: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Device Software Functions: Lifecycle Management and Marketing Submission Recommendations
entries.notes: Draft guidance covering AI-enabled devices across the total product lifecycle (see Healthcare)
entries.applies_to: manufacturers of AI-enabled medical device software seeking FDA marketing submission

entries.document: Good Machine Learning Practice for Medical Device Development: Guiding Principles
entries.notes: Ten joint GMLP principles for AI/ML medical devices; Oct 2021 (see Healthcare)
entries.applies_to: developers of AI/ML-based medical devices following joint FDA, Health Canada, and MHRA principles

entries.document: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI/ML)-Enabled Medical Devices
entries.notes: FDA's list of AI/ML-enabled devices authorized for US marketing (see Healthcare)
entries.applies_to: manufacturers and reviewers tracking AI/ML-enabled devices authorized for US marketing

entries.document: SR 11-7 — Guidance on Model Risk Management
entries.notes: Banking model-risk validation baseline applied to AI/ML models; revised by SR 26-2 (Apr 2026) (see Finance)
entries.applies_to: banking organizations validating and managing risk of AI/ML models under Federal Reserve and OCC supervision

entries.notes: Pharmaceutical Good Manufacturing Practice, EU GMP (EudraLex Volume 4) and GAMP live under the Life Sciences 'Clinical research' page; see its GxP section

pages.title: Sustainability & ESG — reporting frameworks, standards & regulation
pages.intro: A curated index of authoritative, primary-source frameworks, standards, and regulations governing corporate sustainability and ESG reporting. Every link points to the issuing body's own canonical page or document.

entries.notes: Universal, Sector and Topic Standards for impact reporting
entries.applies_to: organizations of any size or sector choosing to report sustainability impacts

entries.notes: 77 industry-specific standards; ISSB maintains and builds on them
entries.applies_to: companies reporting industry-specific, financially material sustainability information

entries.document: IFRS S1 General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-related Financial Information
entries.notes: Effective for periods from 1 Jan 2024
entries.applies_to: entities adopting ISSB sustainability-related financial disclosure requirements

entries.notes: Climate standard building on TCFD; effective from 1 Jan 2024
entries.applies_to: entities adopting ISSB climate-related financial disclosure requirements

entries.notes: Free HTML/PDF access point for the issued ISSB Standards
entries.applies_to: anyone seeking free access to the issued ISSB sustainability disclosure standards

entries.issuer: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
entries.notes: Four-pillar climate disclosure framework; TCFD monitoring moved to IFRS
entries.applies_to: organizations disclosing climate-related financial risks and opportunities

entries.issuer: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
entries.notes: Final Report (2017) and implementation guidance documents
entries.applies_to: organizations implementing TCFD-aligned climate disclosure guidance

entries.notes: Framework for value-creation reporting; hosted by the IFRS Foundation
entries.applies_to: organizations producing integrated reports connecting value creation across capitals

entries.notes: Amends the Accounting Directive; phased reporting from FY2024 (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: large EU companies and listed SMEs subject to phased mandatory sustainability reporting from FY2024

entries.document: European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) — Delegated Regulation (EU) 2023/2772
entries.notes: First set of ESRS in Annexes I–II; applies from 1 Jan 2024 (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: companies in scope of CSRD applying the detailed ESRS disclosure requirements from 1 Jan 2024

entries.notes: EFRAG drafts the ESRS as technical adviser to the Commission
entries.applies_to: stakeholders following EFRAG's technical drafting and advisory work on ESRS

entries.notes: Commission overview of CSRD scope, timeline and ESRS
entries.applies_to: companies and advisers seeking the European Commission's overview of CSRD scope and timeline

entries.notes: Classification framework for environmentally sustainable activities (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: financial market participants and companies classifying environmentally sustainable economic activities

entries.notes: Commission guidance on the taxonomy classification system
entries.applies_to: companies and investors applying the EU Taxonomy classification system

entries.notes: Sustainability-related disclosures in financial services (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: financial market participants and advisers disclosing sustainability-related information in financial services

entries.notes: Commission hub linking taxonomy, disclosures and standards

entries.document: SEC Adopts Rules to Enhance and Standardize Climate-Related Disclosures for Investors
entries.notes: Adoption of the climate rule, 6 Mar 2024; stayed pending litigation (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: SEC-registered public companies subject to climate-related disclosure requirements

entries.notes: Full final rule text; Scope 1/2 and financial-statement effects (bot-blocks WebFetch, canonical URL confirmed)
entries.applies_to: SEC registrants required to disclose Scope 1/2 emissions and financial-statement climate effects

entries.document: SB 253 — Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act
entries.notes: Scope 1–3 GHG disclosure for large companies; approved 7 Oct 2023
entries.applies_to: large companies doing business in California disclosing Scope 1-3 GHG emissions

entries.document: SB 261 — Greenhouse gases: climate-related financial risk
entries.notes: Biennial climate financial-risk reports; approved 7 Oct 2023
entries.applies_to: large companies doing business in California filing biennial climate financial-risk reports

entries.document: GHG Protocol Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard
entries.notes: Foundational corporate GHG inventory standard
entries.applies_to: organizations building corporate greenhouse gas emissions inventories

entries.notes: Value-chain emissions accounting across 15 categories
entries.applies_to: organizations accounting for value-chain (Scope 3) emissions across 15 categories

entries.applies_to: investors incorporating ESG considerations into investment practice
